bio: I'm Ka Vatana, a Software Engineering student from Cambodia training as an AI-native founder-engineer. I learn by building real product surfaces, documenting tradeoffs, and tightening the gap between idea, code, demo, and user trust.

My current work is a private startup portfolio: BayonHub for tech student opportunities, Svaeng Yul for Khmer self-study, Chomkar for agriculture market access, Sat Digital for community cybersecurity, Vantrex for trading tooling, PHSAROS for SME operations, and CHNAI LAB as the student-run product studio behind the work.

I also build personal systems like Studio OS, a local-first life dashboard with a privacy-safe public demo. Those projects help me practice product judgment, offline-first state, strict TypeScript, demo boundaries, and the discipline of shipping proof without leaking private data.

I use AI tools such as Claude, ChatGPT, and Codex-style workflows to research, plan, code, review, and clean up repositories. I do not treat generated code as automatically correct; I verify with audits, type checks, production builds, manual smoke tests, and careful source-boundary decisions.
I'm still early, but I am serious about becoming useful: build the thing, inspect the failure modes, document what is true, and improve the system one verified commit at a time.
aiNote: I use AI tools to help research, plan, code, and review while keeping human judgment, tests, and verification in the loop.
currentFocus: AI-native engineering workflows with human review
Private startup source with safe public demos and case studies
Full-stack fundamentals: TypeScript, Vue/Nuxt, React/Next, APIs, databases, auth, deployment
GitHub hygiene, dependency security, and repository positioning
Cambodia-first product thinking across education, agriculture, SME operations, cyber, and student opportunity infrastructure
Reading, testing, and understanding code instead of only generating it
